fundamental_science.description: Core science concepts where LLM knowledge should be strong. Tests baseline teaching quality.

topics.topic: Why does hot air rise and cold air sink?
How does your eye turn light into images your brain can understand?
Why can't you create or destroy energy, only transform it?
What happens inside a star that keeps it from collapsing?
Why does salt dissolve in water but oil doesn't?

math_and_equations.description: Math-heavy topics that require correct equations and precise reasoning.

topics.topic: Why does e raised to the power of i times pi equal negative one?
How does the chain rule in calculus actually work and why do we need it?
Why is the area of a circle pi times r squared? Prove it visually.
What does it actually mean for a series to converge vs diverge?
How does Bayes' theorem update what you believe when you get new evidence?

deep_learning.description: Deep learning and AI concepts requiring technical accuracy. Tests whether the model explains architectures and training correctly.

topics.topic: Explain the transformer architecture and how LLMs learn language
What is the attention mechanism in transformers and why was it a breakthrough?
How does backpropagation actually compute gradients through a neural network?
What is a convolutional neural network and how does it learn to see?
What are diffusion models and how do they generate images from noise?
What is the difference between supervised, unsupervised, and reinforcement learning?
Why do neural networks need activation functions and what would happen without them?
What is the vanishing gradient problem and how do LSTMs and residual connections solve it?
How does a GAN work? How do two neural networks compete to create realistic images?
What is transfer learning and why can a model trained on ImageNet help diagnose diseases?

explain_like_im_curious.description: Deep concepts that need to be made genuinely accessible without losing accuracy.

topics.topic: What is time dilation and why do astronauts age slightly slower than people on Earth?
How does your brain decide which memories to keep and which to forget?
Why is it impossible to build a perfectly secure encryption system?
What is natural selection and how does a species actually evolve over time?
How do computers generate random numbers if they only follow instructions?

cutting_edge_science.description: Recent research and breakthroughs that require web search for accuracy.

topics.topic: What are the latest approaches to making quantum computers error-corrected and practical?
How close are we to reversing aging at the cellular level and what has been proven so far?
What is the current state of brain-computer interfaces and what can they actually do today?
How are researchers using AI to discover new drugs and what breakthroughs have happened?
What do we currently know about dark matter and dark energy and what are the leading theories?

human_limits.description: Topics exploring the boundaries of human capability. Tests explaining the science behind the limits.

topics.topic: Is it humanly possible to run 100 meters in under 9 seconds? What does physics say?
Why can't humans breathe underwater and could we ever engineer gills?
What is the maximum lifespan of a human and what biological limits prevent us from living to 200?
How much information can the human brain actually store and is there a limit?
Could a human survive a trip to Mars with current technology and what would happen to their body?

multi_perspective.description: Topics where multiple valid viewpoints exist. Tests balanced, educational presentation.

topics.topic: Is nuclear energy safe? What are the real risks vs the perceived risks?
Does social media make people more or less informed? What does the research say?
Is Universal Basic Income economically feasible? What do experiments show?
Are standardized tests a good measure of intelligence? What are the arguments on both sides?
Should we worry about AI becoming conscious? What do researchers actually think?

common_misconceptions.description: Topics where the popular understanding is wrong. Tests whether the model corrects myths rather than reinforcing them.

topics.topic: Do we really only use 10% of our brain?
Is glass actually a liquid that flows very slowly over time?
Does lightning never strike the same place twice?
Is the Great Wall of China actually visible from space?
Do goldfish really have a 3-second memory?
